fleet.title: Fleet
fleet.subtitle: Repo status across machines — local column measured live, remote columns from each machine's last report

fleet.metaStale: Hub unreachable — showing the cached manifest and reports ({{error}})
fleet.branchOffManifest: On a branch the manifest does not manage

auto.neverRun: No automatic round has run yet

manifest.title: Manage fleet manifest
manifest.removeHint: Removing a row only removes it from the manifest; it never deletes a checkout or mirror.
manifest.previewMessage: Review the exact add, update, and remove diff before pushing it to the fleet meta repository.

bootstrap.previewMessage: Review the exact branch, hub commit, and new local path for {{name}} before applying.

reason.repo_not_in_manifest: Repository is not managed by fleet
reason.path_outside_projects_root: Repository path escapes the configured projects root
reason.target_exists_empty: The target path is an empty directory left by a failed bootstrap — remove it to retry
reason.target_unreadable: Could not prove that the target path is absent
reason.hub_name_reserved: origin is reserved for a human-configured upstream remote

chain.rootsHint: Folders scanned for Original Repositories. Scanned in order; duplicate roots are scanned once.
chain.rootsEmpty: No roots configured — using the default.
chain.overviewSubtitle: Patchbay Central or developer checkouts → project .agents/skills → agent entry links; global surfaces stay empty
chain.projectsSubtitle: Per-project skill chains and agent entry status — link and unlink skills for each project

workbench.brokenDesc: {{entry}} points at {{target}}, which no longer exists.
workbench.removeBrokenHint: No candidate target found — this deletes the dangling symlink.
workbench.recordSubtitle: {{count}} link edits, journaled — undo any time.
workbench.recordUndoUnverified: Undo not fully verified — some links changed since the repair; see the record.
workbench.liveNote: Every step is journaled; undo the whole repair any time.
workbench.repoMoveDesc: {{count}} broken links all point into {{oldRoot}} — every skill reappears by name under {{newRoot}}, so this is a whole-repo move.
workbench.dirtyDesc: The original repository has your uncommitted changes — commit to feed them upstream.
workbench.wizardNoSources: No sources available — configure warehouse roots in Settings first.
workbench.wizardSkillsSummary: Will create {{count}} links · next: create the .claude/skills entry and pick agents
workbench.wizardEntryHint: Creates each selected agent's entry as a directory link to the .agents/skills aggregate; existing surfaces get per-skill entries instead.
workbench.wizardPresetMissing: {{count}} preset items are not in the current sources — skipped

remediate.title: Remediate {{name}}
remediate.willRemove: Then retire the global symlink once the project chain verifies.
remediate.willKeep: The global entry will be kept until the project chain verifies.
remediate.unverified: {{name}} could not be verified; the global entry was left in place.

chain.warehouseSubtitle: Patchbay Central is the managed default; optional developer checkouts retain Git health and update actions
chain.duplicatesSubtitle: Checkouts that resolve to the same remote — compare and choose an authority yourself (nothing is deleted)
chain.detailHint: Click a node to inspect its hop-by-hop chain
chain.planHint: Every target path, action, and conflict is listed below. Nothing is written until you apply.
chain.applyUnverified: Applied with issues — review the results below
chain.unlinkConfirm: Unlink {{name}}? Only validated symlinks are removed; the original is untouched.
chain.unlinkSharedWarning: Unlinking {{name}} here is a shared-surface operation — every Agent listed below loses access. The original is untouched.
chain.unlinkUnverified: {{name}} unlink applied but a rescan could not verify it.

doctor.title: Doctor
doctor.subtitle: Findings for topology deviations — filter by severity and type, then repair, ignore, or mark project-private
doctor.clean: No deviations found. Every managed chain follows the convention.
doctor.noMatch: No findings match the active filters.
doctor.repairUnsupported: No longer repairable — rescan and try again.
doctor.repairVerified: Repair verified — the chain is normalized.
doctor.repairUnverified: Repair applied but not verified. Rescan to inspect.

pull.previewHint: Patchbay only fast-forwards clean repositories that are behind their upstream. It never resets, stashes, force-updates, merges, or resolves conflicts.
pull.noEligible: No selected repository is eligible for a fast-forward.

forkSync.previewHint: Fork maintenance: upstream is the source, origin is the target. Patchbay only fast-forwards origin/<branch> up to upstream/<branch>, and refuses divergence, an ambiguous remote, or any non-fast-forward.
forkSync.pushWarning: Applying PUSHES to origin (fast-forward only). It never force-pushes, rebases, merges, or rewrites history.
forkSync.noEligible: No selected fork is eligible for a fast-forward sync.

instructions.costBarHint: Global resident tokens per agent — click for the per-project breakdown

doctor.subtitle: Findings for skill chains and instructions surfaces — share severity filters and filter types by module
doctor.clean: No chain or instructions findings. Every managed surface follows the convention.

normalize.title: Normalize instructions · {{project}}
normalize.planHint: Review every file action and its complete resulting content before applying.

init.title: Initialize instructions · {{project}}
init.planHint: Review every create-only target and its complete content before applying.

help.title: Quick Start for Patchbay
help.description: Start with the project-only chain: Original Repository → project aggregate (.agents/skills) → Agent entry. Global Guard keeps global Agent surfaces empty. Press ⌘K anytime for the command palette.

workflows.title: Project-local three-tier workflow
workflows.description: Use Link Topology to inspect the full chain. In Project Links, register a project, link selected Skills from an Original Repository into its .agents/skills aggregate, then expose that whitelist through each selected Agent entry. Doctor previews and verifies supported repairs.

presets.title: Presets
presets.description: Group skills into named presets. In any workspace, click a Preset pill to instantly activate or deactivate all its skills for the current agent scope. Active presets show ✓; partially installed ones show a count. Applying a preset is a one-time copy — not a live sync.

install.title: Install Skills
install.description: Import from local folders, Git repositories, archives, or the marketplace. Batch-import multiple skills from a folder at once.

sync.title: Library
sync.description: Browse skills in Patchbay Central with source filters, bulk tags, update checks, and deletion. Open a card to view docs and source diffs or link the skill into a registered project; coding-agent global install controls have been removed.

global.title: Global Guard
global.description: Policy-managed global Agent surfaces are compliance inputs, not installation targets. Global Guard scans them read-only; vendor-managed built-ins such as QoderWork are outside the empty-surface policy. Legacy Global Workspace URLs now redirect to Doctor.

projects.title: Project Workspaces
projects.description: Link a project to browse per-agent local skill folders, compare with the central library, and import or export changes. Use Preset pills to activate skill groups, or open + Add Skills to pick from your library with a multi-agent target picker.

backup.title: Backup & Sync
backup.description: Connect a GitHub backup repository once on the Backup page — backups then run automatically, and devices connected to the same repository stay in sync with each other. If the same skill is edited on two devices, it shows up under "Needs attention" with three choices (keep mine / use remote / keep both); a safety snapshot precedes every choice. Secrets and machine-specific settings are never backed up.

settings.title: Settings
settings.description: Manage agent paths and toggles, add custom agents, drag to reorder how agents appear across the app, change sync mode, theme, text size, language, proxy, Git remote, AI search key, and check for app updates. Use Export Logs to bundle recent logs and install/remove/update/sync activity into a single zip when filing an issue.

commandPalette.placeholder: Jump to skill, preset, project, action…
commandPalette.empty: No matches. Try a different query.

dashboard.summary: {{skills}} Skills in Library · {{agents}} Agents connected · {{projects}} Projects

backup.title: Backup
backup.subtitle: Connect once, then back up or restore your skill library from one place.

status.loadingDesc: Reading the local library and remote backup state.
status.notConnectedDesc: Save a backup repository URL, then choose whether to restore from it or back up this machine as new.
status.needsFixDesc: This machine and the backup cannot sync safely until the connection is repaired.
status.pendingSkills: {{count}} skill(s) have changes not yet backed up.
status.remoteOnlyDesc: {{remote}} update(s) were pushed by your other devices. Syncing merges them into this machine — nothing here is uploaded or overwritten.
status.pendingBothDesc: Local changes: {{local}} · remote updates: {{remote}}. Syncing merges both per skill — neither side overwrites the other.

merge.applied: Merged {{count}} update(s) from your other devices.
merge.newConflicts: {{count}} skill(s) were changed on two devices at once. Your local version is kept — review them below.

conflicts.title: Needs attention
conflicts.desc: These skills were changed on two devices at the same time. Your local version stays in place until you choose; a safety snapshot is taken before any choice is applied.
conflicts.blockedBothDevices: The same skill has an unresolved conflict on both devices. Resolve it on either device first, then sync again.
conflicts.autoPaused: Remote updates touching these skills resume automatically after you decide; sync manually to pick everything up now.

connection.title: Connection
connection.desc: Use a custom Git URL for SSH, self-hosted services, or an existing repository.

auto.title: Automatic backup
auto.desc: Back up automatically a few minutes after changes settle; a local save also runs when the app quits.

device.label: This device
device.renamed: Device renamed. Future backups will be signed with the new name.

github.title: Connect with GitHub
github.desc: Patchbay's short-lived credential is server-scoped to the one private backup repository you select. It is stored in the system keychain, never in files.
github.tokenHint: Create a token on GitHub (repo scope, pre-filled)
github.errorToken: GitHub rejected the token. Check that it hasn't expired and was copied completely.
github.errorScope: The token can't create or access the repository. Use a classic token with the "repo" scope, or grant a fine-grained token Administration (write) permission.
github.errorAppRepoAccess: Patchbay can't access that repository. Create or choose the private backup repository, then install the Patchbay GitHub App for that repository only.
github.errorAppRepoNotPrivate: Patchbay won't back up to a public repository. Make the selected repository private, then try again.
github.errorAppInstallationScope: The Patchbay installation isn't limited to exactly this repository. In GitHub App settings, choose "Only select repositories" and leave only this private backup repository selected.
github.errorAppReauth: The Patchbay GitHub authorization expired and couldn't be refreshed. Authorize Patchbay again.
github.errorKeychain: Couldn't store the token in the system keychain. Guided GitHub setup requires the keychain; you can still use the advanced Git URL below.
github.errorAppNotConfigured: The Patchbay GitHub App is not configured in this build. Use a personal access token below instead.
github.stepRepoTitle: Create or choose a private backup repository
github.stepRepoDesc: Use patchbay-backup or enter the name of an existing private repository.
github.stepInstallDesc: Choose "Only select repositories" and grant access only to your backup repository. Patchbay requests Contents read/write, not repository administration.
github.stepAuthorizeDesc: Authorize twice on first setup: Patchbay identifies the private repository, then GitHub issues a final credential locked to that repository.
github.deviceWaitDesc: A browser window opened at {{uri}}. Enter the code above there to authorize — this page will continue automatically.
github.deviceRepositoryScoped: Repository confirmed. Authorize once more so GitHub can lock the final credential to this repository.
github.deviceExpired: The verification code expired. Click "Authorize Patchbay on GitHub" to get a new one.
github.deviceDenied: Authorization was declined on GitHub. Nothing was changed.
github.deviceFallbackPat: If github.com is hard to reach from your network, use a personal access token below instead.
github.patToggle: Use a personal access token instead (advanced)
github.publicRepoWarning: Heads up: the connected backup repository is PUBLIC — anyone can read your skills, tags, and presets. If that's not intended, make it private on GitHub: repository Settings → General → Danger Zone → Change visibility.

history.title: Backup History
history.empty: No visible snapshots yet. Run a backup to create one.

scope.title: Backup Scope

included.metadata: Tags, presets, preset membership, and per-agent skill toggles

excluded.secrets: API keys, tokens, and proxy credentials
excluded.local: Machine-specific paths, app settings, and local agent setup

scope.sizeHint: Very large skill folders may make backups slow. The planned default limit is 100 MB per skill folder, with oversized skills kept local.
scope.repoTooLarge: Backup is {{size}} in total — past 1 GB backups may get slow.
scope.oversizedSkill: "{{name}}" is {{size}} — already backed up; large skills may make backups slow.
scope.oversizedExcluded: "{{name}}" is {{size}} — over the 100 MB limit, kept on this machine and not backed up.

disconnect.title: Disconnect This Machine
disconnect.desc: Removes the backup connection and the access credential saved on this machine only. Local skills and remote backup data are kept.
disconnect.confirmMessage: This removes the saved remote connection and this machine's access credential. Your local skills and remote repository will remain.
disconnect.revokeHintGithubApp: Removing the Patchbay GitHub App installation revokes access to the selected repository for every connected device.
disconnect.revokeHintPat: Deleting the token on GitHub only affects devices using that token.
disconnect.revokeConfirmGithubApp: GitHub's installed-app settings will open — remove or restrict Patchbay there. Connected devices will lose access to the affected repository, and this machine will be disconnected now. Remote backup data is kept.
disconnect.revokeConfirmPat: GitHub's token settings page will open — delete the backup token there. Devices using that token lose access, and this machine will be disconnected now. Remote backup data is kept.
disconnect.revokeHintUnknown: How this machine was connected wasn't recorded — both the app-authorization and token pages will open so you can revoke whichever applies.
disconnect.revokeConfirmUnknown: GitHub's app-authorization and token settings pages will both open — revoke whichever this machine was using. This machine will be disconnected now. Remote backup data is kept.
disconnect.deleteRemoteDesc: Permanently deletes the backup repository on GitHub. Other devices will no longer be able to sync. Skills on this machine are kept.
disconnect.deleteRemoteConfirm: The repository settings page on GitHub will open. Delete the repository in its Danger Zone — GitHub asks you to type the repository name to confirm. Afterwards, other devices will report that the backup no longer exists.
disconnect.deleteRemoteOpened: Repository settings opened in the browser. After deleting, use "Disconnect This Machine" to clear the local connection.

backup.credentialsMigrated: The access token found in your backup URL was moved to the system keychain. It no longer appears in any file.
backup.restoreSafetyPoint: Your previous state was saved as snapshot {{tag}} — you can restore it from the history at any time.

firstRun.title: Patchbay's central library is empty. Restore from backup?
firstRun.subtitle: This only means Patchbay's central library has no skills yet — it does not mean your local source repositories or project skill chains are empty. If another device has a Patchbay backup, paste its repository URL; otherwise, continue without restoring.
firstRun.restoreSuccess: Central-library backup restored — your skills are ready.
firstRun.hint: You can connect or restore a Patchbay central-library backup later from Backup in the sidebar.

mySkills.title: Library
mySkills.applyMissingDefault: No enabled agent installed — apply needs at least one location.
mySkills.applyCallout: Clicking a preset only opens it. Use Apply Preset to add or remove its skills from selected agents.
mySkills.subtitle: {{count}} skills from the central library are enabled in “{{preset}}”
mySkills.searchPlaceholder: Search skills in the central library...
mySkills.deleteConfirm: Delete skill "{{name}}" permanently? This removes its central library files, preset links, and synced agent targets.
mySkills.addFirst: Click "Install Skills" to add skills into the central library
mySkills.noMatch: No skills match the current search or filter
mySkills.syncNothingToDo: This skill already covers every installed agent
mySkills.unsyncNothingToDo: This skill is not synced to any agent yet
mySkills.needsAttentionHint: This skill has a sync conflict — open the Backup page to resolve it
mySkills.gitVersionEmpty: No snapshots yet. Run Sync to create one.
mySkills.gitVersionRestoreConfirm: Restore skill library to {{tag}}? This creates a new restore commit and keeps current history.
mySkills.gitVersionRestoreNeedSync: Restore is local for now. Run Sync to Git to share it with remote.
mySkills.sourceDiffUnavailable: Source content is unavailable right now

sourceDiff.noChanges: No changes — the source matches the central library

summary.too_large: File changed but is too large to preview

tags.deleteConfirm: Remove the tag "{{tag}}" from all skills?

mySkills.batchDeleteConfirm: Delete {{count}} selected skills permanently? This removes their central library files, preset links, and synced agent targets.

noResults.title: No matching market skills

toast.timeout: Installation timed out — check your network

local.title: Import a local skill source
local.description: Choose a folder that contains `SKILL.md`, or import a `.zip` or `.skill` package.

install.gitDesc: Enter a public or private Git repository URL. For private repos, ensure SSH keys are configured.
install.gitAlreadyInstalled: "{{name}}" is already installed. Reinstalling will update it.

gitPreview.title: Select Skills to Import
gitPreview.description: The following skills were found in the repository. Rename them if needed, then confirm.

scan.title: Scan Local Skills
scan.initial: Scan agent skill folders already present on this machine.
scan.summary: Scanned {{tools}} tool folders and found {{skills}} importable skills.
scan.noResultsHint: Everything may already be managed, or those tool folders do not contain skills yet.
scan.listHint: Existing local skills were found and can be imported directly into the central library.
scan.listImportedHint: Everything in this scan is already in the central library.

settings.title: Settings
settings.removeCustomAgentConfirm: Remove custom agent "{{name}}"? Synced skills will be unlinked.
settings.projectSkillsPathDesc: Optional. Set a path relative to the project root to make this custom agent available in project workspaces.
settings.overridePathHint: Using a manually overridden skills path
settings.repoPathDesc: Default storage location for all Skills and preset configs.
settings.repoPathReset: Central repository path reset to default
settings.repoPathRestartNotice: Restart the app to apply the new path and migrate existing data.
settings.repoPathEmpty: Enter a central repository path first
settings.repoPathDefaultHint: Using the default central repository path
settings.repoPathCustomHint: Using a custom central repository path
settings.syncModeDesc: Symlinks are recommended — minimal disk usage with live updates. Applies to both global and project workspaces; note that symlinks in a project point to this machine's central library and won't travel with the repo.
settings.currentPresetDesc: Switch the app to another preset and refresh active skills immediately.
settings.defaultPresetDesc: Preset loaded automatically on app launch.
settings.textSizeDesc: Adjust the app's base font size.
settings.tagline: Cross-platform. Minimal, efficient, seamless multi-preset switching.
settings.restartToApply: Update installed. Restart the app to apply.
settings.reportIssueHint: Copy app/OS info and recent log excerpt to clipboard and open a new GitHub issue
settings.reportIssueExportHint: If the issue is complex, click 'Export Logs' in Settings → About and attach the zip to this issue.
settings.diagnosticsCopied: Diagnostic info copied. Paste it into the GitHub issue.
settings.diagnosticsCopyManual: Could not access clipboard — please copy the info below manually:
settings.exportLogsHint: Bundle recent logs and activity history as a zip in your Downloads folder
settings.exportLogsDone: Logs exported ({{count}} file(s)). Drag the zip into your GitHub issue.
settings.panicBanner: Patchbay crashed during the last session ({{time}}).
settings.repoWarning_config_unreadable: The central library config file is unreadable or corrupt, so this session fell back to the default location (~/.patchbay). If your library looks empty, your data is still at the previously configured path — set “Central Repo Path” back to it.
settings.repoWarning_repo_path_invalid: The configured central repository path is invalid, so this session fell back to the default location (~/.patchbay). If your library looks empty, re-set “Central Repo Path” to where your data lives.
settings.gitBackupDesc: Back up your skill library to a Git repository for version control and multi-machine sync.
settings.proxyUrlDesc: All Git clones and network requests will route through this proxy. Leave blank to connect directly.
settings.proxyUrlInvalid: Proxy URL must start with http://, https://, or socks5://

autoUpdate.title: Skill Auto-Update
autoUpdate.intervalDesc: How often the app checks installed skills for upstream updates in the background.
autoUpdate.applyDesc: When on, updates are pulled and applied automatically; when off, they are only flagged in the Library for manual update.

appUpdate.title: Patchbay App Updates
appUpdate.description: Check for signed Patchbay releases once a day after startup. Updates are installed only when you approve and restart.
appUpdate.installFailed: Could not install the update. Use the download link in Settings instead.
appUpdate.noLongerAvailable: That update is no longer available.

settings.gitSyncConfigDesc: Remote and connection management now live only on Backup; advanced engine choices remain here.
settings.gitDisconnected: Backup disconnected. Local skills and remote data are kept.
settings.gitDisconnectHint: Disconnect removes the remote configuration from this machine only. Local skills and the remote repository are kept.
settings.gitEngineGit2Desc: Use the app's built-in Git engine for HTTPS backup network operations — no system git needed, credentials injected from the keychain. SSH and custom remotes always use system git. Turn off to revert at any time.
settings.gitMergeEngineObjectDesc: Default. Merges synced changes per skill instead of per text line: renames combine with edits, and conflicting skills are kept locally and listed on the Backup page for review instead of blocking the sync. Turn off to fall back to the legacy line-based git merge.
settings.gitStartHint: If Remote URL is filled, it will clone from remote; otherwise it initializes a local repository.
settings.gitSetupSubtitle: Choose how this skill library connects to a backup.
settings.gitSetupCardCloneDesc: Clone from the configured remote. Local skills are preserved and non-conflicting items are merged back.
settings.gitSetupCardInitTitle: Back up this machine as new
settings.gitSetupCardInitDesc: Initialize Git locally and push the current skill library to the configured remote.
settings.gitSetupCardInitBadge: First time: when remote is empty
settings.gitSetupNeedRemote: Set a backup repository URL first.
settings.gitRecoverySubtitleUnrelated: Local repository and remote do not share history; sync cannot proceed. Pick how to fix it.
settings.gitRecoverySubtitleNoUpstream: No upstream tracking is set up with the remote. Pick how to fix it.
settings.gitRecoverySubtitleDetached: Currently in detached HEAD state, possibly after restoring a snapshot. Realign to remote or restore a different snapshot first.
settings.gitRecoverySubtitleConflict: Local and remote skills were changed in conflicting ways. The merge was rolled back automatically — re-clone from remote to restore a clean copy. Skills that exist only locally are preserved.
settings.gitRecoveryCardRecloneDesc: Keep a temporary copy of local skills, re-clone from remote, then merge non-conflicting items back. Safest option.
settings.gitRecoveryCardKeepLocalDesc: Try to push local content as the new remote head. Only use when remote is empty or local should overwrite remote.
settings.gitRecoveryFallbackHint: "Keep local" currently requires terminal action. See docs or contact the developer.
settings.gitNeedRemoteSetup: Set a backup repository URL first
settings.gitNeedPullFirst: Remote has newer changes. Pull first to avoid conflicts.
settings.gitPendingSync: Local changes are ready to sync
settings.gitErrorAuth: Authentication failed. Check SSH key or access token.
settings.gitErrorNetwork: Network connection failed. Check your network and remote URL.
settings.gitErrorConflict: Local and remote changes conflict. Choose how to resolve it.
settings.gitErrorNotRepo: Git repository not found. Set up backup first.
settings.gitErrorRejected: Remote has new updates. Pull and merge them before syncing.
settings.gitErrorUnrelatedHistories: Local and remote do not share history. Open Fix Sync Setup to resolve.
settings.gitErrorNoUpstream: No upstream tracking is set up. Open Fix Sync Setup to resolve.
settings.gitErrorGeneric: Git operation failed. Check remote URL in Settings and try again.
settings.closeActionDesc: Choose what happens when you click the window close button.
settings.trayIconDesc: Show an icon in the system tray for quick Show and Quit actions.
settings.trayIconOffHint: Tray icon is hidden, so closing the window will quit the app.

closeAction.title: Close behavior
closeAction.message: Minimize to tray keeps the app running in the background and lets you reopen it from the tray icon.
closeAction.remember: Remember my choice for next time

common.dataOutOfDate: Some data could not be refreshed

project.title: Project Skills
project.workspaceHint: Manage local agent skills in this repository and sync them with the Skills Center.
project.linkedWorkspaceHint: Manage skills in this linked external workspace without turning it into a global sync target.
project.noSkillsHint: No agent skills directories found. Create a skills directory (e.g. .claude/skills/) and add skill folders.
project.scanDirPlaceholder: Enter root directory to scan, e.g. ~/Work
project.scanNoResult: No projects with agent skills directories found
project.removeConfirm: Remove project "{{name}}" from the list? Project files will not be deleted.
project.invalidDir: Directory does not contain any agent skills
project.addLinkedHint: Link an external skills root. It will be managed as a workspace only and will not participate in global preset sync.
project.linkedDisabledPathHint: A sibling directory with the "-disabled" suffix will be created automatically for disabled skills.
project.addCallout: You can also add multiple skills at once — filter by tag in the Add Skills dialog.
project.exportTagFilterHint: Pick one or more tags to bulk-add related skills (e.g. "frontend" + "debug").
project.selectSkillToExport: Select a skill to add to this project
project.searchCenterSkills: Search skills in the central library...
project.selectTargetAgents: Select at least one target agent
project.agentPickerHint: Enabled agents are shown first; others stay collapsed
project.agentAddRequiresCenter: {{agent}} can only be added after this skill is synced to the Skills Center
project.noSkillsToExport: No skills available to add to this project
project.deleteSkillConfirm: Permanently delete skill "{{name}}"? This action cannot be undone.
project.batchDeleteConfirm: Permanently delete {{count}} selected skills? This action cannot be undone.
project.batchUpdateCenterFailed: {{count}} skills failed to update to Skills Center
project.batchUpdateProjectFailed: {{count}} skills failed to update in project
project.batchTagsFailed: {{count}} center skills failed to update tags

presetActions.addHelp: Adds missing skills only. Existing skills are skipped.
presetActions.removeHelp: Removes selected matching skills from the selected agents only.
presetActions.addSummary: {{count}} skill-agent additions ready; {{skipped}} existing will be skipped.

lobsterWorkspace.title: Lobster Agents
lobsterWorkspace.noAgentsHint: Install OpenClaw, Hermes Agent, or another lobster-class personal assistant to see it here.

globalWorkspace.title: Global Workspace
globalWorkspace.subtitle: Skills installed globally for each agent
globalWorkspace.noAgentsHint: Configure agent paths in Settings to get started.
globalWorkspace.noSkillsForAgent: No skills installed. Click Add Skill to install from the library.
globalWorkspace.allInstalled: All library skills are already installed for this agent
globalWorkspace.batchRemoveConfirm: Remove {{count}} selected skills from {{agent}}? This only unlinks them from the global workspace; central library files are kept.

localSkills.deleteLocalConfirmMessage: Delete "{{name}}" from {{agent}}? This removes the local files and cannot be undone.
localSkills.uploadedToast: Uploaded "{{name}}" to Skills Center. It was not synced back to {{agent}}.
localSkills.uploadConfirmMessage: Upload "{{name}}" to the matching Skills Center entry? This replaces the center copy.
localSkills.pullConfirmMessage: Pull the Skills Center version of "{{name}}" into {{agent}}? This overwrites the local agent copy.
